codeReview.summary: Core OAuth domain matching and pending-member flow are in place, but membership status transitions are overly permissive in the new admin endpoints.

issues.description: Approve/reject endpoints call OrganizationMembershipUpdateStatus directly by org+user without requiring current status='pending'. An org admin can therefore reject or reactivate any member (including already-active users), which violates the intended 'pending member' lifecycle and can revoke active access unexpectedly.

OrganizationFindByEmailDomain uses LIMIT 1 while schema only adds a non-unique index on organizations.email_domain (schema.sql line 45). If multiple orgs claim the same domain (e.g., concurrent first logins), matching becomes nondeterministic and may route users to the wrong org.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission guidance is internally contradictory about schema edits, which caused avoidable ambiguity for this feature.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Mission AGENTS.md line 9 says 'DO NOT modify the PostgreSQL database schema' and line 14 marks internal/database/schema.sql off-limits, but line 48 explicitly says schema changes ARE allowed for OAuth domain matching.

sharedStateObservations.observation: security-backend-worker skill conflicts with mission reality for OAuth features that require schema work.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/security-backend-worker/SKILL.md line 82 says to return to orchestrator when schema changes are required ('not authorized for this mission'), while this reviewed feature explicitly required schema additions.

summary: Review failed: pending-member approval/rejection endpoints are not scoped to pending memberships and can alter active memberships. Also noted a non-blocking domain-claim determinism risk and shared-state documentation contradictions around schema-change policy.
